feclftwmRRROT TTfe I
If the Sun be screened on the right side by clouds that are
white or white-fringed, there would be rain. The same result would
ensue, if he be screened likewise by clouds which resemble bushes
of Andropogon grass, and which arise in an unblasted (not heated
by the Sun) quarter.
[The metre is this and the following two verses].
ftTcf: 1
A white cross-bar of clouds appearing at sunrise will be disas-
trous to the king; a blood-red one will ruin the army through
internal dissensions; and one with a golden hue will bestow pros-
perity and victory on the army.
If there be mock suns touching the sun on both sides, there
would be abundant rain.
